zotero.abstractNote: Over-parameterization has been shown to be effective for training deep neural networks. However, it often leads to redundant parameters and unstable training. In this paper, we propose Orthogonal Over-Parameterized Training (OOPT), a novel training framework that encourages the weights of over-parameterized layers to be orthogonal. We show that OOPT can effectively reduce the redundancy of over-parameterized layers and improve the training stability. We also provide a theoretical analysis of the proposed method and demonstrate its effectiveness on various computer vision tasks.
zotero.conferenceName: IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR)
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Proceedings of the IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR)
